entry.title: STATE POLICE JAIL MAN FOLLOWING FREEWAY INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:20 p.m. Troopers with Oregon State Police jailed a man following a freeway incident on Wednesday morning. An OSP report said at 10:45 a.m. a trooper responded after a caller said there was a subject in the southbound lanes of travel near Rice Hill. The adult male was allegedly observed lunging at...

entry.title: Winston Dillard Water District lifts boil water notice
entry.summary: WINSTON — The Winston Dillard Water District has lifted the boil water notice issued late Monday afternoon.

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.12.26
entry.summary: Republican candidate for Governor, Chris Dudley talks about his vision for the state and what his priorities would be if elected.

entry.title: Douglas County Senior Services to hold informational series events across the county
entry.summary: DOUGLAS COUNTY — Douglas County Senior Services has begun a countywide Senior Resource Series aimed at connecting older adults, caregivers and families with locally available services and supports.

entry.title: Tribal-owned pharmacy in Roseburg offers care for all locals
entry.summary: Amid a wave of pharmacy closures in the state, a new facility is opening its doors in Roseburg, offering much-needed services to the community.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CITE MAN FOR PROHIBITED CAMPING
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 10:50 a.m. Roseburg Police cited a man for prohibited camping early Thursday morning. An RPD report said just after 2:00 a.m. officers located a large camp on the gravel road between Northeast Casper Street and Northeast Fulton Street. A 24-year-old was found inside the camp and was given the citation before being...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANTS
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 10:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for warrants on Wednesday afternoon. An RPD report said just after 5:30 p.m. the 40-year-old was contacted near the intersection of Northwest Estelle Street and Northwest Garden Valley Boulevard and was detained on his warrants. Bail was set at $11,250.

entry.title: Tribe celebrates opening of community pharmacy
entry.summary: The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of Indians celebrated a milestone in community health care Wednesday with a ribbon-cutting ceremony at the new Cow Creek Health & Wellness Pharmacy in Roseburg.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL WOMAN FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 10:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a woman for a failure to appear warrant on Wednesday. An RPD report said at 6:30 p.m. officers contacted the 47-year-old in the 500 block of Northeast Garden Valley Boulevard and learned she had an unworked warrant out of RPD. The woman was detained on $20,000...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR ALLEGED PRIOR THEFT INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 10:10 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man early Thursday morning for an alleged prior theft incident. An RPD report said at 1:00 a.m. officers contacted the 37-year-old regarding the theft of a $3,500 trailer from a store in the 3300 block of Northwest Aviation Boulevard on March 3rd. The man admitted to...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL WOMAN FOR ALLEGED TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 10:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a woman for an alleged trespass incident on Wednesday evening. An RPD report said at 10:30 p.m. the 40-year-old was contacted after she allegedly refused to leave a city approved campsite at the west end of the Washington Avenue Bridge in the 300 block of Washington Avenue....

entry.title: Douglas County woman charged for stealing puppies from camp
entry.summary: A Douglas County woman is in legal trouble after being charged with stealing seven 12-day-old puppies and their mother.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 9:50 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a warrant on Wednesday. An RPD report said at 11:30 a.m. officers contacted the 46-year-old who was walking on Northeast Chestnut Avenue near the intersection of Northeast Walnut Street. He was arrested for a warrant out of Roseburg Municipal Court. Bail was set at...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR ALLEGED PAST THEFT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 9:40 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man Wednesday for an alleged past theft. An RPD report said at 12:45 p.m. officers stopped a vehicle for traffic violation in the 700 block of Northwest Garden Valley Boulevard. The passenger was arrested for the prior incident. He was taken into custody and transported to...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL TWO PEOPLE FOLLOWING TRAFFIC STOP
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed two people for drug law violations on Wednesday evening. An RPD report said at 8:20 p.m. an officer contacted a 23-year-old woman on a traffic stop near the intersection of Southeast Lane Avenue and Southeast Fullerton Street. The officer reportedly saw a Ziploc baggy of a crystal-like...

entry.title: Police Log: March 11, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported multiple arrests and citations on today’s log page, including warrant arrests, drinking in public, camping violations, trespassing, meth-related charges, a DUII case, and a theft arrest tied to a stolen trailer.

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.12.26
entry.summary: Umpqua Community College Small Business Center Director Aaron Larsen talks about the Umpqua Economic Development Partnership Growth Talks Pitch Night events returning next month.

entry.title: Roseburg Schools discuss budget priorities with board of directors
entry.summary: As staff in Roseburg Schools get started on the budget for next year, Superintendent Jared Cordon asked for input on what the district’s board of directors think the priorities should be next.

entry.title: Veterans bring stories, information, advice to students at Sutherlin High School
entry.summary: SUTHERLIN — The Sutherlin High School gymnasium was alive with the sounds of students questioning the walking and talking history books in their midst.

entry.title: Kitten Returned After Theft From Winston Rescue, Woman Arrested
entry.summary: A kitten stolen earlier this week from FCAT Rescue & Thrift Shop in Winston was safely returned Thursday, and a woman was arrested after surveillance video helped identify the suspect, according to the rescue’s founder.

entry.title: Fox & Fiddle small business collective to celebrate grand opening in Roseburg
entry.summary: In the past, the one similarity between Fox & Fable Bookshop, Happy Belly Bakery, Heart Beet Homestead and Pickering Pantry was that they were small-scale businesses without a storefront. This weekend, they get to celebrate something new they have in…

entry.title: $450,000 Allocated To Support Glide Revitalization Center Project
entry.summary: A $450,000 state funding allocation will support renovations at the Glide Revitalization Center, expanding access to child care, community space, library resources, social services and other programs intended to serve rural residents in and around Glide.

entry.title: March 12, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: DAYS CREEK SCHOOL DISTRICT SUPERINTENDENT CONFIRMS RETIREMENT
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:50 a.m. Days Creek School District Superintendent Joe LaFountaine has announced his plans to retire at the end of June. LaFountaine told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that in 2023 he came out of retirement to join the district as their interim superintendent. LaFountaine said he deeply enjoyed the community...

entry.title: UPTD BOARD TO MEET ON MONDAY
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Umpqua Public Transportation District Board will meet on Monday night, March 16th. Agenda items include a look at the Accident and Incident Response Policy and Procedures. The board will consider a vehicle replacement plan and a passenger survey. General Manager and Chief Financial Officer, Sheri Bleau will look at...

entry.title: TRIBE CELEBRATES OPENING OF COMMUNITY PHARMACY
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:30 a.m. The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of Indians celebrated a milestone in community healthcare on Wednesday with a ribbon-cutting ceremony at the new Cow Creek Health & Wellness Pharmacy in Roseburg. A Tribal release said the event highlighted a landmark step in the Tribe’s commitment to improving access to...

entry.title: AVIVA HEALTH TO HOST THIRD ANNUAL SHAMROCK QUEST
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:20 a .m. Aviva Health will host the third annual Shamrock Quest this Saturday, March 14th from 12:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. An Aviva release said the free community event offers an afternoon of family friendly activities designed to connect residents with local organizations while promoting wellness and outdoor fun for all...

entry.title: ROSEBURG HIGH SCHOOL BANDS TO HOLD SPRING CONCERT NEXT WEEK
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 3:05 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: The Roseburg High Schools bands will have their spring concert next Monday night, March 16th. The event will begin at 7:00 p.m. in the Rose Theater on the RHS campus on West Harvard Avenue. It will feature the Concert, Jazz, and Percussion Ensembles. For those...

entry.title: UMPQUA VALLEY FARM TO SCHOOL COMMUNITY GARDEN WORKDAY ON FRIDAY
entry.summary: March 12, 2026 2:50 a.m. Umpqua Valley Farm to School will have a community garden workday on Friday. It will take place from 3:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. in the garden located at 1450 Southeast Mill Street. All ages and abilities are welcome to help out. Tools will be provided. For more information, email: erin@uvf2s.org...

entry.title: Cow Creek Umpqua Tribe Opens New Pharmacy in Roseburg, Expanding Local Access to Care
entry.summary: Cow Creek Umpqua Tribe opened a new community pharmacy Wednesday in Roseburg, expanding local access to prescription services and integrated care for Tribal citizens and other patients through Cow Creek Health & Wellness clinics.

entry.title: 3rd Annual Shamrock Quest Set For March 14 In Roseburg
entry.summary: Families across Douglas County are invited to the 3rd Annual Shamrock Quest on Saturday, March 14, from noon to 3 p.m. at Aviva Health in Roseburg. The free event will feature games, activities, food trucks, prizes and community resource booths.

entry.title: CANYONVILLE WOMAN JAILED FOLLOWING ALLEGED DUII CRASH
entry.summary: March 11, 2025 2:00 p.m. A Canyonville woman was jailed following an alleged DUII crash on Tuesday afternoon. An Oregon State Police report said just before 4:15 p.m. a trooper responded to the single-vehicle wreck into a guardrail on Interstate Five northbound, a mile south of Myrtle Creek. While enroute, dispatch advised that the vehicle...

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 3.11.26
entry.summary: State Representative Alek Skarlatos shares his impressions on wins and losses from the recent short session of the Oregon State Legislature.

entry.title: Roseburg Woman Jailed After Police Say She Took Seven Puppies And Their Mother From Camp
entry.summary: Roseburg police said a 36-year-old woman was jailed Tuesday night after allegedly taking seven puppies and their mother from a camp near West Stanton Street. Officers said the dogs were returned to their owner, and the woman was booked on eight counts of first-degree theft.

entry.title: Rural Oregon benefits: Glide receives $450k for community center renovation
entry.summary: Oregon House Bill 5204 has allocated $450,000 to Glide Revitalization, a nonprofit in Glide, to renovate the Glide Revitalization Center, according to state legislature officials.

entry.title: Roseburg Man Jailed After Police Recover Reported Stolen Pickup
entry.summary: Roseburg police said a 36-year-old man was jailed Tuesday after officers found him in a reported stolen Ford F-150 in a Safeway parking lot. Police said he admitted taking the truck but claimed he had permission from its previous owner.

entry.title: Tickets still available for Hops for K9 Cops
entry.summary: ROSEBURG — Tickets are still available for the Hops for K9 Cops event set for 2 p.m. Sunday.

entry.title: Vietnam Veterans of America welcome home event to be held at fairgrounds
entry.summary: ROSEBURG — The Vietnam Veterans of America will have a welcome home event March 25.

entry.title: WINSTON-DILLARD WATER DISTRICT LIFTS BOIL WATER NOTICE
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 11:05 a.m. The Winston-Dillard Water District has lifted the boil water notice issued late Monday afternoon. District Manager Tanner Pence said test results of samples submitted show that they are all clear of contaminates. All restrictions were lifted effective mid-morning Wednesday. Pence said district customers had been asked to boil their water...

entry.title: TWO VEHICLES SUSTAIN HEAVY DAMAGE IN HIGHWAY CRASH
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 10:50 a.m. Two vehicles sustained heavy damage in a highway crash on Tuesday afternoon. An Oregon State Police report said just after 4:20 p.m. troopers along with Glide Rural Fire Protection District and the Oregon Department of Transportation responded to the two-vehicle wreck on Highway 138E, approximately six miles west of Glide....

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR DRIVING WHILE SUSPENDED
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 10:25 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for driving while suspended on Tuesday. An RPD report said just before 12:00 p.m. officers stopped the 60-year-old traffic violations near the intersection of Northeast Garden Valley Boulevard and Northeast Cedar Street. During the stop, it was determined that the man was suspended at the...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL WOMAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 10:10 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a woman for a warrant early Wednesday morning. An RPD report said at 6:00 a.m. the 43-year-old was contacted as she was camping in the back of a vehicle which was parked in the 700 block of Northwest Hill Avenue. Officers knew the woman had an unworked...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 10:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a warrant early Tuesday. An RPD report said at 3:15 a.m. the 40-year-old was contacted during a traffic stop at the intersection of Southeast Mosher Avenue and Southeast Flint Street. Officers learned the man had an active warrant for his arrest. He was taken...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL WOMAN FOR THEFT OF PUPPIES

An RPD report said just after 8:40 p.m. officers contacted 36-year-old Kayla Jones in the 900 block of West Stanton Street. Jones had allegedly called Animal Control in regard to her finding seven puppies and their mother...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOLLOWING TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 9:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man following a trespass incident on Tuesday. An RPD report said 9:30 a.m. the 33-year-old returned to a store in the 2500 block of Northwest Stewart Parkway after being trespassed. The man acknowledged that he knew he was not allowed to be at the store. His...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CHARGE MAN WITH UNAUTHORIZED USE OF A MOTOR VEHICLE
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police charged a man with unauthorized use of a motor vehicle on Tuesday morning. An RPD report said a victim said his vehicle had been stolen in the early morning hours on Monday from a residence in the 2000 block of Northeast Stephens Street. The keys were not in...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.11.26
entry.summary: Miss Douglas County 2026 Jasman Brar talks about her life, her career plans, her love for flying planes, and what is ahead this year as Miss Douglas County.

entry.title: Police Log: March 10, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg Police’s March 10 log included a recovered stolen truck, a DUII case, camping and burn citations, a trespass arrest, and a theft case involving seven puppies and their mother, according to the daily police log.

entry.title: Oregon Naturalist Field Program To Explore Southwest Oregon Landscapes
entry.summary: The Oregon Naturalist Southwest Oregon field experience will take participants across the Umpqua Basin and Klamath-Siskiyou region from April through September, offering expert-led excursions focused on ecology, wildlife, geology and natural history.

entry.title: American Red Cross Highlights Volunteer Opportunities During Red Cross Month
entry.summary: March is Red Cross Month, and the American Red Cross is encouraging people in Southwest Oregon to volunteer. Open roles include disaster response, shelter support, blood donor ambassador and home fire campaign team member positions.

entry.title: Days Creek Charter School's LaFountaine looks toward returning to retirement
entry.summary: It took him a little bit to make up his mind but Days Creek Charter School Superintendent Joe LaFountaine decided to retire again after 42 years working in Oregon schools.

entry.title: Community Garden Work Day Planned March 13 In Roseburg
entry.summary: A community garden work day is planned for Friday, March 13, in Roseburg. Volunteers are invited to help restore the garden from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m. at 1450 SE Mill St. Organizers said tools and supplies will be provided, and all ages and abilities are welcome.

entry.title: March 11, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: NEAR RECORD HIGH TEMPERATURES POSSIBLE EARLY NEXT WEEK
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 6:20 a.m. Near record high temperatures are possible in the Umpqua Valley early next week. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the Roseburg will be on the south end of a warm front next Monday through Wednesday. High temperatures of 73...

entry.title: Roseburg families engage in learning through rhythm, rhyme and sound
entry.summary: The Rose Elementary School campus in downtown Roseburg was alive Monday afternoon with the sounds of children and their families exploring the world of rhythm, sounds and meaningful play.

entry.title: UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE ANNOUNCES PHARMACY TRANSFER PARTNERSHIP WITH PACIFIC UNIVERSITY
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 4:00 a.m. Umpqua Community College has launched a new partnership with Pacific University designed to help students pursue careers in pharmacy through a streamlined transfer pathway to a doctoral degree program. A UCC release said that through the agreement, students at UCC and Chemeketa Community College will be able to complete...

entry.title: CITY OF ROSEBURG HAS COMMISSION OPENINGS
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 3:50 a.m. The City of Roseburg has commission opening on three of its commissions. A city release said applications are being taken from people interested in filling vacancies on the Budget Committee, the Economic Development Committee and Planning Commission. The Planning Commission has two vacancies while the other commissions each have one....

entry.title: VIETNAM VETERANS OF AMERICA WELCOME HOME EVENT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Vietnam Veterans of America will have a welcome home event on March 25th. A Roseburg VA Medical Center release said it will take place at 11:00 a.m. in the Community Conference Hall of the Douglas County Fairgrounds. Doors will open at 10:00 a.m. Door prizes will be given out,...

entry.title: TICKETS STILL AVAILABLE FOR HOPS FOR K9 COPS EVENT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 3:30 a.m. Tickets are still available for the Hops for K9 Cops event this Sunday afternoon at 2:00 p.m. It is presented by Friends of Umpqua Valley Police K9 Programs and will be held at Backside Brewing on Northeast Odell Avenue in Roseburg. Attendees can meet the K9 teams, see demonstrations and...

entry.title: TICKETS NOW ON SALE FOR THE REVOLTIONISTS AT UACT
entry.summary: March 11, 2026 3:20 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Tickets are now on sale for The Revolutionists at Umpqua Actors Community Theatre in Roseburg. The Revolutionists is a new play about four very real women who lived boldly in France during the Reign of Terror of the French Revolution. Described as a “grand and...

entry.title: Judge Upholds $77,767 In Winchester Dam Repair Penalties
entry.summary: A judge upheld $77,767 in penalties against the Winchester Water Control District over water quality violations tied to 2023 repair work at Winchester Dam on the North Umpqua River, according to a final order issued March 6.

entry.title: Pineapple Express on the way for the end of the Week
entry.summary: #PineappleExpress developing. Minor flooding issues will likely develop by FRIDAY for areas of the northern Willamette Valley and Northern Coast. Temps will also be warming with rising snow levels up to 7,000-FT on THURSDAY.Heaviest rain start times- Portland area WEDNESDAY,…

entry.title: ROSEBURG FIRE DEPARTMENT RESPONDS TO FIRES NEAR THE DISC GOLF COURSE
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:00 p.m. Crews with the Roseburg Fire Department responded to two fires near the Disc Golf Course, adjacent to the bike path, just south of the Roseburg VA Medical Center, early Tuesday morning. According to Administrative Battalion Chief Chris Galligan, crews were dispatched just before 7:15 a.m. to the fires which were...

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.10.26
entry.summary: Live from Salem, State Senator David Brock Smith talks about the positives and negatives of the Special Session of the legislature, and his recently announced campaign for U.S. Senate, hoping to challenge Senator Jeff Merkley.

entry.title: Roseburg man accused of firing at vehicles on Interstate 5, leading deputies on pursuit into Winston
entry.summary: A Roseburg man faces multiple charges after authorities say he fired at vehicles on Interstate 5 Saturday night before leading police into Winston, where deputies used a spike strip to stop him. One driver reported a bullet shattered his truck’s back window.

entry.title: Umpqua, Chemeketa community colleges initiate pathway with Pacific University for pharmacy degree
entry.summary: A new agreement will allow Umpqua Community College students to pursue a career in the pharmacy through a partnership with Pacific University.

entry.title: Roseburg man charged for shooting cars on I-5 in Winston chase
entry.summary: A man from Roseburg faces serious charges after allegedly shooting at cars on Interstate 5.

He was directed...

Officers reviewed video footage and the 57-year-old was allegedly seen taking the wallet. The man...

entry.title: Drinking water warning in effect in Winston
entry.summary: WINSTON — A drinking water warning is in effect for the next 24 hours in Winston following a loss of water pressure in the distribution system Monday. A notice from the Winston Dillard Water District states potentially harmful bacteria could…

After an investigation, it was determined that the first...

entry.summary: March 10, 2026 9:30 a.m. Roseburg Police have added a new charge to a man already in jail. An RPD report said information came in regarding a past theft at a business in the 1300 block of Northwest Garden Valley Boulevard. The 37-year-old was developed as a suspect and was allegedly observed on video stealing...

entry.summary: March 10, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a parole violation on Monday. His parole officer authorized a detainer and was...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.10.26
entry.summary: Elena Lininger from Source One Serenity and William Wagner from the Roseburg VA talk about the public screen of the documentary HERE.IS.BETTER on March 21st at the Roseburg Public Library.

entry.title: Police Log: March 9, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported six incidents Monday, including a theft, camping violations, a parole violation, an illegal burn, and a traffic crash involving a suspended driver, according to the City of Roseburg Police Logs.

entry.title: Abacela Winery's 2025 Grenache Rosé is the Greatest of the Grape
entry.summary: CANYONVILLE — Abacela Winery, HillCrest Winery, Trella Vineyards and Freed Estate Winery came away with awards Saturday night when the Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association held the Greatest of the Grape at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville.

entry.title: Roseburg VA Health Care System Executive Director Patrick Hull retires
entry.summary: For over 30 years, Patrick Hull has served in the health care industry, providing leadership and management to health care projects and organizations across the country. This month, he has retired from his most recent position: executive director of the…

entry.title: March 10, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: VERY LIGHT RAIN AMOUNTS EXPECTED THIS WEEK
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 4:00 a.m. Very light rainfall amounts are expected in the Umpqua Valley for much of this week. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the Roseburg area is stuck at the top of a high-pressure system. That means that moisture in the...

entry.title: LENTEN MEDITATIONS IN MUSIC WITH UCC CHAMBER CHOIR ON FRIDAY
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:50 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Lenten Meditations in Music continues on Friday at First Presbyterian Church on Southeast Lane Avenue in downtown Roseburg. Held for decades, the annual series is designed to provide time for gathering and prayerful contemplation during Lent. The Umpqua Community College Chamber Choir directed by Dr....

entry.title: BENTZ INTRODUCES LEGISLATION TO STOP MISUSE OF ENDANGERED SPECIES ACT
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:40 a.m. On Monday, Second District Congressman Cliff Bentz introduced legislation to stop application of the provisions of the Endangered Species Act to the National Flood Insurance Program. A Bentz release said the legislation amends the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 so that the program remains focused on its intended purpose:...

entry.title: RETAINING WALL CONSTRUCTION WILL DELAY TRAFFIC ON HIGHWAY 38
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:30 a.m. Covering coastal Douglas County: Drivers can expect traffic delays on Highway 38 east of Reedsport next week due to a new highway project. Dan Latham of the Oregon Department of Transportation said starting Monday, March 16th, contractors will build a retaining wall around the inlet of a box culvert at...

entry.title: COW CREEK HEALTH & WELLNESS CENTER PHARMACY OPENING WEDNESDAY
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:20 a.m. The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of Indians will open its Cow Creek Health & Wellness Pharmacy on Wednesday. A Tribal release said that with dwindling pharmacy options in Roseburg over the past few years, the Cow Creek Tribal Government is thrilled to offer a new and convenient option...

entry.title: ABACELA WINERY WINS TOP HONORS AT GREATEST OF THE GRAPE
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 3:05 a.m. Abacela Winery in Winston won top honors at the 55th annual Greatest of the Grape held Saturday night at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville. A release from the Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association said the event brought together more than 400 guests to celebrate wines from across the Umpqua Valley....

entry.title: 4-H BRUSHSTROKES OF SPRING: PAINTING WORKSHOP
entry.summary: March 10, 2026 2:50 a.m. Douglas County 4-H is partnering with Create & Sip of Roseburg to host 4-H Brushstrokes of Spring. An Extension release said this will be a hands-on painting workshop where youth will explore their creativity, practice painting techniques and enjoy a fun, interactive art experience. The workshop is perfect for youth...

entry.title: KQUA Expands Community Radio Reach Across The Umpqua Valley With New 90.5 FM Signal
entry.summary: KQUA is expanding community radio across much of Douglas County with its new 90.5 FM signal. A public soft-launch celebration is set for March 12 in downtown Roseburg, where organizers hope listeners, volunteers and future hosts will help shape the station’s future.

entry.title: TRUE WEALTH 03.09.26
entry.summary: Presented by Littlejohn Financial, David Littlejohn and Emma Arndt have our weekly local financial program.

During contact with the man, he was...

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.09.26
entry.summary: State Representative Virgle Osborne takes a look at the legislation considered during the short session of the Oregon State Legislature.

entry.title: CASA Of Douglas County To Host Coffee Meet-And-Greet In Reedsport
entry.summary: CASA of Douglas County will host a free coffee meet-and-greet from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. Tuesday, March 10, at Highwater Cafe & Market in Reedsport, where community members can meet Executive Director Angela Gross and learn how to become a child advocate.

entry.title: Police Log: March 6 - March 9, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported dozens of incidents from March 6-9, including DUII arrests, warrants, thefts, trespass cases, prohibited camping citations, crashes and disorderly conduct, according to the City of Roseburg Police Logs service.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR ALLEGED TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 10:50 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for an alleged trespass incident on Sunday. An RPD report said at 11:45 a.m. the 34-year-old returned to a business in the 2400 block of Northwest Stewart Parkway after being previously trespassed. The man was located a short distance way and was taken into custody...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR REPORTED TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 10:40 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a reported trespass incident on Saturday morning. An RPD report said at 6:00 a.m. officers contacted the 33-year-old after employees at a store in the 900 block of Northwest Garden Valley Boulevard said the man was in the parking lot harassing customers. The suspect...

entry.title: ROSEBURG WOMAN JAILED FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 10:20 a.m. A Roseburg woman was jailed for a warrant by the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office on Sunday night. A DCSO report said at 10:30 p.m. a caller said there was a disturbance in the 100 block of Trout Loop in the Green District. The caller said another woman had locked her...

entry.title: CANYONVILLE AREA TRANSIENT JAILED FOR RESTRAINING ORDER VIOLATION
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 10:10 a.m. A Canyonville area transient was jailed for a restraining order violation on Friday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at 10:30 a.m. 51-year-old Sean Kelley was arrested in the 200 block of Main Street in Riddle. Kelley allegedly had contact with the victim the previous night at her workplace....

entry.title: SUTHERLIN POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 9:50 a.m. Sutherlin Police jailed a man for a warrant on Friday night. An SPD report said at 9:20 p.m. officers contacted the 29-year-old in the 800 block of East Central Avenue. It was found that he had a valid misdemeanor warrant for his arrest out of Douglas County Circuit Court. The...

entry.title: OSP JAILS MAN FOLLOWING REPORTED FREEWAY MENACING INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 9:40 a.m. Troopers with the Oregon State Police jailed a man following an alleged freeway menacing incident on Saturday night. An OSP report said just after 9:20 p.m. a reckless driver was called in for being unable to maintain its lane while headed southbound in the Curtin/Lorane area. Shortly after, a second...

entry.title: ROSEBURG MAN JAILED FOR 14 COUNTS OF RESTRAINING ORDER VIOLATION
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 9:20 a.m. A Roseburg man was jailed on Sunday afternoon for 14 counts of a restraining order violation by the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office. A DCSO report said at 4:30 p.m. a caller indicated that 38-year-old Joshua Cetina was at a residence in the 400 block of Longview Drive in Elkton, which...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.09.26
entry.summary: Jake Tranter of Spiderking Studios talks about the Student Creator Project public service announcement being premiered on Wednesday evening at Roseburg Cinema.
